title: Unpinned filesystem server over broad home paths is BLOCK

origin.description: Shape-preserving redaction of a real committed .mcp.json from the public repo WinshipWheatley/openclaw-eyes (RC-B09, 0.3.0-rc.0 window). The repo carries no detectable license, so only the non-copyrightable config shape is retained: an unpinned 'npx -y @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem' granted two absolute /home/<org> paths. The paths use the project's own public org name and depth (which drives the broad-path verdict); they were left as-is per the RC log, with no personal identifiers present.
redactions: license omitted: source repo has no detectable license; stored as a shape-preserving redacted snapshot, not redistributed verbatim

humanExpectation.rationale: A filesystem server is granted broad absolute home-directory roots (/home/openclaw/docs/*), which is a blocking broad-path surface, and the package is launched unpinned via 'npx -y'. Broad filesystem reach handed to an autonomous tool is the blocking condition; the unpinned package is an additional supply-chain finding.
knownLimitations: The broad-path detector reads the server's own absolute path args; scoping these to a workspace-relative subpath and pinning the package would move the verdict toward REVIEW/SAFE.
